content: The 2024-2025 SOTA push for scalable active learning agent memory can be reframed as an episodic pruning problem, where transformer-based episodic memory agents actively select only high-surprise, high-curiosity past trajectories to retrain on instead of storing all episodic traces. This creates a curious active loop where the agent’s own learned curiosity signal prioritizes which episodic memories get added to the active learning fine-tuning queue, eliminating redundant low-value memory bloat.
